sections.paragraph:
Μεσοποταμία, ας, ἡ (subst. fem. of μεσοποτάμιος, α, ον=‘located betw. rivers’. ἡ μεσοποταμία, sc. χώρα=‘the land betw. rivers’ [Polyb. 5, 44, 6; Strabo 11, 12, 2], esp. that betw. the Euphrates and Tigris) Mesopotamia (Diod S 2, 11, 2; 18, 6, 3; Appian, Syr. 48 §246; 53 §269; Arrian, Anab. 3, 7, 3; 7, 7, 3; Polyaenus, Exc. 9, 2; Ptolem., Apotel. 2, 3, 22; 28; OGI 54, 18; LXX; Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 1f Jac.; Philo, Joseph.; TestJud 9:1; 10:1) Ac 2:9. In the narrative about Abraham (cp. Gen 24:10) 7:2.—LDillemann, Haute Mésopotamie orient. et pays adjacents, ’62; Pauly-W. XV 1105–63; Kl. Pauly III 1237–41.

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

fem. fem. = feminine

betw. betw. = between

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

esp. esp. = especially

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Ptolem Ptolem , math., II a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 634
